title: ANCHOR_CHAIN_QUERY
description: Request from the Referee to a Keeper for the anchor-chain ledger of a specific terminal within a time window. Where VERIFICATION_QUERY checks the existence of hashes the Referee already knows (point verification), ANCHOR_CHAIN_QUERY discloses what was anchored in the window (ledger disclosure): it is the basis for evidence_sufficiency coverage and gap evaluation, for retrieving the governing SESSION_START Norm declaration (RFC-0001 Section 9.3), and for PoHI anchor-density evaluation (RFC-0001 Section 8). The query MUST be signed; the Keeper MUST verify the signature against the registered public key of requester_id and MUST reject the query unless requester_id is the Referee bound to incident_id via INCIDENT_NOTICE (INCIDENT_OPEN), or requester_id equals target_terminal_id (self-query).

incident_id.description: Identifier of the incident this query is issued for. Used by the Keeper for authorization: the requester must be the Referee bound to this incident (or the target terminal itself).

requester_id.description: Terminal ID of the requester. Either the Referee assessing incident_id, or the target terminal querying its own chain.

target_terminal_id.description: Terminal ID of the party whose anchor chain is requested. The Keeper MUST restrict the result to records submitted by this terminal.

range.description: Time window of interest. The Keeper returns anchors whose Keeper-recorded timestamps fall within [start, end], plus the governing SESSION_START (see ANCHOR_CHAIN_RESULT). The Referee MAY widen the window to perform deeper chain verification itself.

timestamp.description: Timestamp of when this query was sent.

signature.description: Ed25519 signature by the requester over the canonical body excluding this field. Verified by the Keeper against the registered public key of requester_id.
